ogTitle: Doodenanning vs Dorre Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Doodenanning and Dorre Island.
heading: Doodenanning vs Dorre Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Doodenanning, Western Australia and Dorre Island, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dorre Island has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Doodenanning (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Doodenanning or Dorre Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Doodenanning has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Dorre Island scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Doodenanning scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Dorre Island scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Doodenanning or Dorre Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dorre Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
